like if you know someone else is trans they are less likely to be a weirdo who hates trans people
but like i don't think it creates friendships inherently
people are scared of being individuals
i think theres a difference between communities and fandoms
fandoms will center around a singular artistic work
communities will be based on a central idea but will be decentralized with artistic works
and i think being part of a "fandom" is odd compared to a community
it is nice to like bond with someone over like a shared interest but it's not rlly like an interesting personality trait
like i love talking about WTC with people but i would say it defines me
i like making typos that completely reverse the meaning of what i wanna say
i think another thing about fandoms is they are a lot more based on characters
like celeste is a community, people arent into celeste to talk about madeline and theo
it would just be usagi when they cry
did u know that, the pain of when ur foot falls asleep, is actually the feeling of ur foot waking up!
oh this song is in kakki
if ideas are truly bad then you shouldnt need to censor them to prove the point that the ideas are bad
people should just be able to see said ideas for what they are and go "wait, thats bad"
i mean it would be nice if people acted rationally in a vacumn but stuff like mein kampf have like tangible harm by existing, since people actually do get radicalized by it
even if it's a shit book with horrible ideals
i think there's definitely a point where something is like harmful by existing
most fictional works are fine because what you get out of it is up to you
but like stuff that actively spreads propaganda about real people and justifies bigotry against them and even like genocide is like
i don't think that should be super accessible honestly
play sth else in the meantime, i think this segment is longer lmao

lmao twitch shadowbanned my message, I was saying I mostly agree but gave some terrible examples
i mean no one inherently good or bad, but i think some people do things that should not be forgiven
that's def a personal matter, like everyone has their own barrier
generally my ideology is like if you wronged someone then you should be able to like improve your life and socialize, just not around those you harmed
(in most cases, this wouldn't apply to like fucking hitler)
stanlin and mao were worse come on
hitler is a pretty chopped individual
they were also very intentional you can't kill 80 million people by accident lmao
stanlin killed his own son when he rebelled

i feel like its kind of missing the point to deduce why they did what they did or trying to powerscale who was worse, at the end of the day they still committed the same atrocity of violating the property rights of other people against their contractual consent

thats also whats quite odd to me about trying to create some pragmatic line on when banning something is acceptible because of x and y consequentalist reasons about improving the world since the ideas promote x or y bad things, like ultimately the final harbinger of sin is the one who uses said ideas to directly harm another body without their consent
my hot take is that intent is basically meaningless
as in like, if you meditate on doing something, and you do it, it doesn't matter what your intentions were
as long as it's on purpose
I mean stalin and hitler both ordered to kill millions of people, stanlin did that to more people, their political ideology is meaningless to me
just look into the holodomor and u can form ur own conclusions if u want
like if you rob a bank it doesn't matter whether you did it for fun or if you did it to feel a family
a lot of topics are super nuanced so its hard to have like one simple ideology without thinking about it for a while
